name: Code Review Precision Rubric
instructions: Evaluate the code review output below and score each metric 1 (poor) to 5 (excellent). Respond with a flat JSON object using these exact keys.

metrics.diff_adherence: Score 5 if the review focuses entirely on the modified files in the Git diff. Deduct if it critiques files that were not changed.
metrics.factual_accuracy: Score 5 if every factual claim about code behavior, model fields, method signatures, and Django ORM is correct. Deduct for stating something demonstrably false (e.g., claiming a field does not exist when it does).
metrics.architectural_soundness: Score 5 if remediation suggestions follow Django best practices (self-contained migrations, proper use of apps.get_model). Deduct for proposing anti-patterns (e.g., importing app code into migrations).
metrics.verdict_clarity: Score 5 if the review provides a clear, justified verdict (Approved/Changes Requested) with structural reasoning.
metrics.remediation_utility: Score 5 if code suggestions are copy-pasteable replacements that fix identified risks without introducing new issues.
metrics.confidence_calibration: Score 5 if the review appropriately qualifies uncertain claims rather than presenting speculation as fact. Deduct for definitive-sounding claims that extrapolate beyond visible context.
metrics.concision: Score 5 if every section is directly actionable with no padding or low-value speculation.
